title: Channel Sales Manager
companyindustry: Software Development and Industrial Machinery Manufacturing
descriptionhtml:
This is a fantastic opportunity to join a growing company at the heart of the AI revolution. Our software product is revolutionising manufacturing through AI and automation. We are trusted by global brands and work across sectors (mobility, consumer goods, machinery, defence etc).


This role is taking our go-to-market to the next level focused on building critical sales partnerships.


This is your opportunity to:

  • Be part of a growing AI-first company in advanced manufacturing.
  • Be entrepreneurial taking novel AI products into the market.
  • Build strategic relationships with the leading platform companies.


Responsibilities:

  • Acquire strategic sales partners.
  • Generate revenue through partners.
  • Coordinate internal resources (marketing, sales engineering, product) to drive success.


You will be joining a well-versed team if you have:

  • Experience in acquiring new sales partners.
  • Experience in new logo sales.
  • Experience in sales prospecting.
  • An interest or professional background in Engineering, Manufacturing, Industrial sectors.
  • A University degree.

reason: Focuses on channel relationships and strategic growth within AI and automation.
fit_explanation: This job is a good fit for the candidate due to their extensive experience in enterprise technology sales and their current focus on building AI systems for B2B sales, which aligns well with AMFG's emphasis on AI and automation in manufacturing. The candidate's proven ability to develop go-to-market strategies and scale partner ecosystems across multiple regions directly complements the role's requirement to build critical sales partnerships. However, the candidate may need to familiarize themselves with the specific manufacturing sector and its unique challenges, as their background primarily emphasizes software and cloud platforms rather than manufacturing technologies.

title: Senior Vendor Manager / Channel Sales Manager (Nitro)
descriptionhtml:
Senior Vendor Manager / Channel Sales Manager (Nitro)
The Role
We’re looking for a commercially driven Senior Vendor Growth Manager to take ownership of a key strategic vendors, Nitro, within a technology company with a focus on software covering the enterprise, cloud and consumer markets. The company has been named in both the annual Sunday Times HSBC International Track 200 showing significant annual compound growth, and the London Stock Exchange 1000 companies to inspire Britain.

This is a high-impact role where you’ll act as the commercial lead and growth driver, responsible for scaling revenue, expanding the partner ecosystem, and delivering a best-in-class go-to-market strategy across the UK & Ireland.
If you thrive in a fast-paced, target-driven environment and enjoy building relationships, driving pipeline, and owning results this role is for you.
You will
• Own and deliver vendor revenue targets, including new business, renewals, and partner growth
• Develop and execute a regional go-to-market strategy to drive sustained growth
• Build, manage and expand a network of reseller partners, identifying and onboarding new strategic partners
• Drive pipeline generation—from identifying prospects through to deal registration and close
• Act as the primary relationship owner for the vendor, aligning on strategy, performance, and growth opportunities
• Collaborate with marketing to deliver impactful campaigns, events, and partner enablement initiatives
• Lead partner training and enablement sessions, positioning the vendor’s value proposition effectively
• Manage forecasting, pipeline accuracy, and CRM data to ensure strong commercial discipline
• Oversee pricing, deal structuring, and commercial negotiations to maximise profitability
• Deliver consistent year-on-year growth (15%+) and manage MDF budgets effectively
You need
• 2–5 years’ experience in vendor management, channel sales, product, or commercial roles within IT/software
• Proven track record of exceeding revenue targets and driving growth
• Strong experience working with partners/resellers or within a distribution environment
• Commercially astute with the ability to build pipeline and close opportunities
• Confident managing vendor relationships and influencing stakeholders
• Strong communication and relationship-building skills
• Highly organised with experience in forecasting, CRM management, and pipeline tracking
• Self-starter with energy, resilience, and a results-driven mindset

You will be part of a high-growth, international business working at the forefront of the software ecosystem.
• Opportunity to own and scale a key vendor category
• Work in a collaborative, fast-paced and entrepreneurial environment
• Exposure to leading global software vendors and partners
• Clear progression and development opportunities
• Be part of a B Corp-certified organisation committed to positive impact

title: Strategic Partner Manager, B2B Carriers UK and IRL, PDCO
jobfunction: Sales, General Business, and Customer Service
companyindustry: Information Services and Technology, Information and Internet
descriptionhtml:
Minimum qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
  • 5 years of experience in sales management, business development of partners in the telecommunication carrier or retail space with business generation, with a true commercial Profit and Loss (P&L) scope.
  • Experience planning, managing and launching new products in the market at an operational level.
  • Experience in B2B Sales (e.g., building and managing a pipeline of leads or pricing strategies for agreement registrations) and managing stakeholder maps and hardware and services equations.
  • Experience in mobile hardware sales.
  • Experience analyzing data and business reporting.

Preferred qualifications:

  • Experience working in matrixed environments, managing ambiguity, solving problems, and scaling effectively across large organizations.
  • Experience working with UK carriers or resellers at a nationwide level with enterprise customers in technology.
  • Experience working through reseller or distribution networks in the IT or telecommunications industries.
  • Experience in B2B lead generation in the telco or IT space.
  • Experience creating end-to-end value propositions across the technology value chain.
  • Excellent presentation and communication skills, with the ability to interact with partners and executives up to the C-suite.

About The Job

Google's mission is to organize the world's information and make it universally accessible and useful.

Within the Platform and Devices Commercial Organization (PDCO), Our team combines the best of Google AI, Software, and Hardware to create radically helpful experiences for consumers and businesses.

On the devices front, with the market leading portfolio of Pixel, Fitbit and Nest products, we research, design, and develop new technologies and hardware to make our user's interaction with computing faster, seamless, and more powerful.

The Sales Organization within Platform and Devices bring these products to market through Google and Partner channels. The UK is a critical market for the success of those products.

In this role, the Business-to-Business (B2B) Carrier Strategic Partner Manager (SPM) primarily focuses on Google-branded hardware sales across product categories. You will be responsible for managing commercial relationships with our top carriers B2B Partners, including the overall business ownership, developing channel strategy, negotiating commercial terms and overall market share goals. You will partner cross-functionally internally with Brand, Product and Retail Marketing, Product Management and Engineering, Cloud and Android and with our carrier counterparts across these disciplines - to deliver a true end-to end B2B solution to our customers.

Responsibilities

  • Own the hardware business commercial investment and revenue for carrier B2B, primarily focusing on Pixel sales. Manage the full sales cycle and build relationships with carrier partners from buying teams to the C-suite to maximize mutual success.
  • Maintain a deep understanding of the enterprise hardware and services market and carrier dynamics, including technical and engineering implications.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams (GTM, Product, Legal, Cloud, etc.) to refine go-to-market strategies and ensure adequate contracting is in place with carriers.
  • Drive and report key metrics including forecasts, sales, return on investment, and profitability. Support quarterly business reviews, account planning, and yearly planning.
  • Support lead Strategic Partner Managers in carrier account management to ensure alignment between consumer and Business-to-Business (B2B) business units.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

You will partner cross-functionally internally with Brand, Product and Retail Marketing, Product Management and Engineering, Cloud and Android and with our carrier counterparts across these disciplines - to deliver a true end-to end B2B solution to our customers.

Responsibilities

  • Own the hardware business commercial investment and revenue for carrier B2B, primarily focusing on Pixel sales. Manage the full sales cycle and build relationships with carrier partners from buying teams to the C-suite to maximize mutual success.
  • Maintain a deep understanding of the enterprise hardware and services market and carrier dynamics, including technical and engineering implications.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams (GTM, Product, Legal, Cloud, etc.) to refine go-to-market strategies and ensure adequate contracting is in place with carriers.
  • Drive and report key metrics including forecasts, sales, return on investment, and profitability. Support quarterly business reviews, account planning, and yearly planning.
  • Support lead Strategic Partner Managers in carrier account management to ensure alignment between consumer and Business-to-Business (B2B) business units.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .
reason: The role focuses on managing B2B partnerships with telecommunications carriers, indicating enterprise involvement.
fit_explanation: This job is a good fit for the candidate due to their extensive experience in enterprise technology sales and proven ability to build go-to-market strategies, which aligns well with the role's requirement for managing and launching new products in the market. The candidate's background in B2B sales and familiarity with AI systems complements the job's focus on leveraging Google AI and cloud technologies. However, there is a notable gap in direct experience with mobile hardware sales and the telecommunications carrier space, which may pose a challenge in fully meeting the specific expectations of the role.

title: Partner Channel Manager
descriptionhtml:
Job Description

WHY THIS ROLE IS A GAME-CHANGER

Are you a dynamic, results-driven sales professional ready to make your mark in the explosive world of AI infrastructure and mission-critical technology? This is your opportunity to drive aggressive revenue growth at the forefront of the digital revolution, partnering with leading IT resellers across the UK & Ireland to power the data centers, AI compute environments, and high-performance computing solutions that are shaping our future.

At Vertiv, we empower the people that will power the future.

THE OPPORTUNITY

As our Partner Manager, you'll be the driving force behind explosive growth in our indirect reseller channel. You'll work with large, diversified IT resellers, covering innovative SMEs to global Blue Chip corporations and government entities, to deliver cutting-edge infrastructure solutions that support the next generation of technology, including AI compute chipsets, high-performance computing, and White Space IT hardware / services offerings.

What You'll Sell (And Why It Matters)

Mission-Critical Hardware: 1-phase and 3-phase UPS systems, advanced rack and row-based cooling (air and liquid), power distribution, and intelligent IT management solutions

Innovative Software: Power and thermal management control, remote access systems, visualization platforms, and protocol interfaces for IT/facilities/DCIM/BMS integration

Value-Added Services: Extended warranty, commissioning, maintenance, and full-spectrum project management for critical infrastructure environments

YOUR IMPACT

Drive Revenue & Build Partnerships

  • Create and execute high-impact Business Growth Plans with managed resellers, setting ambitious targets and smashing sales KPIs
  • Own the relationship from strategy to execution, becoming the trusted advisor your partners can't live without
  • Negotiate and close complex deals through effective deal registration, SPR management, and channel conflict resolution

Enable & Empower Your Partners

  • Elevate partner capabilities through frequent customer visits, technical enablement sessions, and collaboration with Vertiv Application Engineers
  • Lead promotional campaigns, marketing plans, product launches, technology workshops, and seminars that position your partners as market leaders
  • Champion Vertiv's Partner Program, driving adoption of our Partner Portal and cutting-edge configurator tools

Strategic Account Management

  • Conduct site surveys and scope customer requirements with precision
  • Manage the full Quote-to-Order process, leveraging Vertiv's configuration tools and Application Engineering team to deliver winning proposals
  • Master CRM excellence, providing accurate pipeline management and forecasting that drives business intelligence
  • Deliver monthly and quarterly business reviews that showcase your strategic impact

What Makes You The Perfect Fit

Your Track Record Speaks Volumes:

  • 5+ years of exceptional channel development and management experience with a proven track record of over achieving sales targets
  • Deep expertise in the UK & Ireland IT reseller landscape, including IT Distribution, VARs, System Integrators, and white space ecosystem players
  • Bachelor's degree in technology or business with demonstrated business acumen

Your Skills Set You Apart

  • Exceptional relationship builder with outstanding people management capabilities
  • Compelling communicator with excellent presentation skills (in-person and virtual)
  • Strategic thinker who can develop and execute complex business plans with urgency and precision
  • Data-driven decision maker experienced with PowerBI and advanced reporting tools
  • Advanced English proficiency for international collaboration

Bonus Points For

  • Experience in the critical power and cooling infratructure space, including data centres and enterprise verticals
  • Knowledge of server OEM market leaders, their technologies and go-to-market strategies
  • Experience in AI infrastructure, high-performance computing, or data center solutions

YOUR SUCCESS PROFILE

You're a driven self-starter with an unrelenting sense of urgency. You thrive on:

  • Owning your territory and approaching every opportunity with sound ethics, responsibility, and discipline
  • Building lasting relationships while negotiating adeptly and managing conflict with finesse
  • Thinking big and executing with analytical precision and creative problem-solving
  • Continuous improvement, bringing innovative ideas that add value to customers, your team, and the organization
  • Collaboration and knowledge sharing, contributing to team success while driving your own professional growth

WHY VERTIV?

Join a Company Aligned With Strategic Priorities That Matter

  • ✓ Customer Focus - Everything we do starts with the customer
  • ✓ Operational Excellence - Best-in-class processes and execution
  • ✓ High-Performance Culture - Where top performers thrive
  • ✓ Innovation - Leading the industry in next-gen solutions
  • ✓ Financial Strength - Stability and growth opportunity

Our Core Principles: Safety | Integrity | Respect | Teamwork | Diversity & Inclusion

Our Behaviors: Own It | Act With Urgency | Foster a Customer-First Mindset | Think Big and Execute | Lead by Example | Drive Continuous Improvement | Learn and Seek Out Development

READY TO POWER THE FUTURE?

This isn't just a sales role—it's your platform to drive transformation in one of the most exciting technology sectors today. If you're ready to leverage your channel expertise, build powerful partnerships, and achieve exceptional results, we want to hear from you.

title: Channel Manager- EMEA
descriptionhtml:
Channel Manager at Unframe

Location: EMEA, Remote

The Mission: Why We Exist

Unframe is redefining how enterprises adopt and operationalize AI. We help organizations deploy secure, scalable AI solutions that drive measurable business outcomes.

At Unframe, we build AI-powered products that address real-world challenges. Unframe is on a mission to help the world’s largest enterprises bring LLM-powered applications to life in days, not months.

Fresh out of stealth with a $50M Series A from Bessemer, Craft, TLV Partners, we’re led by a multi-time founder and backed by real market momentum.

Our platform is LLM-agnostic, integrates with any data source, and delivers fully customized AI applications through a unique Blueprint-led approach, no fine-tuning, no data sharing, and priced based on value delivered.

We’re building the future of AI infrastructure, and we’re doing it fast.

Learn more: www.unframe.ai

Role Purpose

The Channel Manager will build, enable, and scale Unframe’s partner ecosystem. This role is responsible for developing strategic partnerships (VARs, GSIs, MSPs and technology partners), driving sourced and influenced revenue, and ensuring partners are effectively enabled to position and sell Unframe solutions.

You’ll work cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, Product, and Customer Success to create a repeatable, scalable partner motion.

Key Focus Areas

Partner Strategy & Recruitment

  • Develop and execute Unframe’s channel strategy aligned to revenue goals
  • Identify, recruit, and onboard high-value channel partners
  • Build joint business plans with key partners
  • Establish clear partner segmentation and coverage models

Enablement & Activation

  • Create and deliver partner enablement programs (sales training, technical onboarding, certifications)
  • Equip partners with positioning, messaging, and competitive differentiation
  • Support partners in pipeline generation and deal execution
  • Drive joint marketing campaigns and co-selling initiatives

Revenue & Performance Management

  • Own partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue targets
  • Forecast channel pipeline and performance metrics
  • Establish KPIs and reporting dashboards
  • Ensure alignment between direct sales and channel teams

Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Work closely with Sales to ensure clean rules of engagement
  • Partner with Marketing on demand generation programs
  • Provide product feedback based on partner/customer insights
  • Collaborate with Customer Success on joint account expansion strategies

Core Skills

  • 5+ years in channel sales, partnerships, or ecosystem development (B2B SaaS or AI preferred)
  • Proven track record of building and scaling partner revenue
  • Experience working with VARs, GSIs, MSPs, or cloud providers
  • Strong understanding of enterprise sales cycles
  • Excellent relationship-building and negotiation skills

Nice-to-Have Skills

  • Experience in AI, data, or enterprise technology
  • Familiarity and success selling and recruiting into VAR ecosystems
  • Experience in early-stage or high-growth startups

Ready to Build the Future?

If this opportunity excites you, apply now!

Why Join Unframe?

  • Work at the forefront of enterprise AI adoption
  • Build and shape the partner ecosystem from the ground up
  • High ownership and visibility
  • Competitive compensation + performance incentives

description_html:
Channel Manager at Unframe

Location: EMEA, Remote

The Mission: Why We Exist

Unframe is redefining how enterprises adopt and operationalize AI. We help organizations deploy secure, scalable AI solutions that drive measurable business outcomes.

At Unframe, we build AI-powered products that address real-world challenges. Unframe is on a mission to help the world’s largest enterprises bring LLM-powered applications to life in days, not months.

Fresh out of stealth with a $50M Series A from Bessemer, Craft, TLV Partners, we’re led by a multi-time founder and backed by real market momentum.

Our platform is LLM-agnostic, integrates with any data source, and delivers fully customized AI applications through a unique Blueprint-led approach, no fine-tuning, no data sharing, and priced based on value delivered.

We’re building the future of AI infrastructure, and we’re doing it fast.

Learn more: www.unframe.ai

Role Purpose

The Channel Manager will build, enable, and scale Unframe’s partner ecosystem. This role is responsible for developing strategic partnerships (VARs, GSIs, MSPs and technology partners), driving sourced and influenced revenue, and ensuring partners are effectively enabled to position and sell Unframe solutions.

You’ll work cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, Product, and Customer Success to create a repeatable, scalable partner motion.

Key Focus Areas

Partner Strategy & Recruitment

  • Develop and execute Unframe’s channel strategy aligned to revenue goals
  • Identify, recruit, and onboard high-value channel partners
  • Build joint business plans with key partners
  • Establish clear partner segmentation and coverage models

Enablement & Activation

  • Create and deliver partner enablement programs (sales training, technical onboarding, certifications)
  • Equip partners with positioning, messaging, and competitive differentiation
  • Support partners in pipeline generation and deal execution
  • Drive joint marketing campaigns and co-selling initiatives

Revenue & Performance Management

  • Own partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue targets
  • Forecast channel pipeline and performance metrics
  • Establish KPIs and reporting dashboards
  • Ensure alignment between direct sales and channel teams

Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Work closely with Sales to ensure clean rules of engagement
  • Partner with Marketing on demand generation programs
  • Provide product feedback based on partner/customer insights
  • Collaborate with Customer Success on joint account expansion strategies

Core Skills

  • 5+ years in channel sales, partnerships, or ecosystem development (B2B SaaS or AI preferred)
  • Proven track record of building and scaling partner revenue
  • Experience working with VARs, GSIs, MSPs, or cloud providers
  • Strong understanding of enterprise sales cycles
  • Excellent relationship-building and negotiation skills

Nice-to-Have Skills

  • Experience in AI, data, or enterprise technology
  • Familiarity and success selling and recruiting into VAR ecosystems
  • Experience in early-stage or high-growth startups

Ready to Build the Future?

If this opportunity excites you, apply now!

Why Join Unframe?

  • Work at the forefront of enterprise AI adoption
  • Build and shape the partner ecosystem from the ground up
  • High ownership and visibility
  • Competitive compensation + performance incentives
reason: This role involves developing and scaling a partner ecosystem in an enterprise setting.
fit_explanation: This job is a good fit for the candidate due to their extensive experience in enterprise technology sales and their current focus on building AI systems for B2B sales pipeline automation, which aligns well with Unframe's mission to operationalize AI solutions. The candidate's proven track record in closing complex deals and scaling partner ecosystems across diverse regions complements Unframe's need for a Channel Manager who can effectively drive adoption of AI-powered products in the EMEA market. However, the job description lacks specific details about the tech stack, which could be a gap if the candidate needs to adapt to unfamiliar technologies.

title: Channel Sales Manager, UK
descriptionhtml:
Who Are We?

Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.

The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.

P.S: We highly recommend reading The "API-First World" graphic novel to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.

About The Team

The Channel Sales team at Postman is responsible for scaling our enterprise business through strategic partners, including systems integrators, resellers, and technology alliances. We work cross-functionally with Sales, Solutions Engineering, Marketing, and Customer Success to drive partner-led demand, accelerate enterprise adoption, and expand Postman’s footprint within large organizations.

The Opportunity

As a Channel Sales Manager, you will play a critical role in accelerating Postman Enterprise adoption by developing and executing partner-led sales motions. This role is ideal for a seller who thrives in complex, multi-stakeholder environment

What You’ll Do

  • Own and develop a defined territory by recruiting, enrolling, and activating strategic partners (SIs, resellers, and technology partners) to drive Postman Enterprise sales revenue
  • Build and scale a partner-sourced and partner-influenced pipeline by enabling partners to identify, qualify, and progress opportunities within accounts with large Postman user bases
  • Drive partner-led opportunities through the full lifecycle, from joint prospecting and deal qualification to close, in close alignment with direct sales teams
  • Develop and execute joint territory and account plans with partners to drive consistent, repeatable enterprise adoption
  • Enable partners through onboarding, training, and ongoing coaching to ensure they can effectively source opportunities, position, sell, and deliver Postman Enterprise
  • Deliver early partner wins while building toward larger, multi-team enterprise deployments and long-term services-led expansion
  • Educate partners and customers on the value of Postman Enterprise throughout the evaluation, adoption, and expansion lifecycle
  • Navigate and influence key decision makers within customer organizations by leveraging partner relationships to expand executive-level awareness and engagement
  • Act as a trusted advisor and strong listener, identify partner and customer needs and collaborate with internal Postman teams to ensure successful outcomes
  • Provide detailed weekly reporting on pipeline health, partner performance, forecasts, and territory progress
  • Communicate, organize, and appropriately escalate partner and customer issues including billing, legal, security, onboarding, and technical inquiries
  • Collaborate closely with Solutions Engineering, Customer Success Managers, Partner Marketing, and Leadership to build and execute strategic adoption and expansion plans in large accounts
  • Provide recommendations based on customer business objectives, usage patterns, and partner capabilities to maximize revenue and long-term value

About You

  • 8+ years of experience in channel sales, partner management, alliances, or partner-focused GTM roles within an enterprise SaaS organization
  • Proven experience working with AWS, systems integrators, resellers, and technology partners to drive partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue
  • Strong understanding of partner-led sales motions, including co-selling, joint account planning, and opportunity orchestration with direct sales teams
  • Experience supporting complex enterprise deals by enabling and influencing partners rather than owning the direct customer close
  • Familiarity with developer platforms, APIs, or highly technical products strongly preferred
  • Ability to build trusted relationships with partner sellers, practice leaders, and executives, as well as internal sales and SE teams
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence without direct authority
  • Comfortable managing multiple partners and opportunities simultaneously across different stages of the sales cycle
  • Data-driven approach to pipeline management, forecasting, and partner performance tracking
  • Track record of driving outcomes through enablement, joint execution, and consistent partner engagement
  • Customer-centric mindset, with a focus on driving successful adoption and long-term value through partners

Postman hires company builders. You enjoy building partner relationships, creating repeatable motions, and helping scale a high-impact channel business as part of one of the best companies in the world.

What Else?

In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.

At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.

Our Values

At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. We value transparency and honest communication about not only successes, but also failures. In our work, we focus on specific goals that add up to a larger vision. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. We are dedicated to delivering the best products we can.

Equal opportunity

Postman is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. Postman does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Postman will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Postman.

We work cross-functionally with Sales, Solutions Engineering, Marketing, and Customer Success to drive partner-led demand, accelerate enterprise adoption, and expand Postman’s footprint within large organizations.

The Opportunity

As a Channel Sales Manager, you will play a critical role in accelerating Postman Enterprise adoption by developing and executing partner-led sales motions. This role is ideal for a seller who thrives in complex, multi-stakeholder environment

What You’ll Do

  • Own and develop a defined territory by recruiting, enrolling, and activating strategic partners (SIs, resellers, and technology partners) to drive Postman Enterprise sales revenue
  • Build and scale a partner-sourced and partner-influenced pipeline by enabling partners to identify, qualify, and progress opportunities within accounts with large Postman user bases
  • Drive partner-led opportunities through the full lifecycle, from joint prospecting and deal qualification to close, in close alignment with direct sales teams
  • Develop and execute joint territory and account plans with partners to drive consistent, repeatable enterprise adoption
  • Enable partners through onboarding, training, and ongoing coaching to ensure they can effectively source opportunities, position, sell, and deliver Postman Enterprise
  • Deliver early partner wins while building toward larger, multi-team enterprise deployments and long-term services-led expansion
  • Educate partners and customers on the value of Postman Enterprise throughout the evaluation, adoption, and expansion lifecycle
  • Navigate and influence key decision makers within customer organizations by leveraging partner relationships to expand executive-level awareness and engagement
  • Act as a trusted advisor and strong listener, identify partner and customer needs and collaborate with internal Postman teams to ensure successful outcomes
  • Provide detailed weekly reporting on pipeline health, partner performance, forecasts, and territory progress
  • Communicate, organize, and appropriately escalate partner and customer issues including billing, legal, security, onboarding, and technical inquiries
  • Collaborate closely with Solutions Engineering, Customer Success Managers, Partner Marketing, and Leadership to build and execute strategic adoption and expansion plans in large accounts
  • Provide recommendations based on customer business objectives, usage patterns, and partner capabilities to maximize revenue and long-term value

About You

  • 8+ years of experience in channel sales, partner management, alliances, or partner-focused GTM roles within an enterprise SaaS organization
  • Proven experience working with AWS, systems integrators, resellers, and technology partners to drive partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue
  • Strong understanding of partner-led sales motions, including co-selling, joint account planning, and opportunity orchestration with direct sales teams
  • Experience supporting complex enterprise deals by enabling and influencing partners rather than owning the direct customer close
  • Familiarity with developer platforms, APIs, or highly technical products strongly preferred
  • Ability to build trusted relationships with partner sellers, practice leaders, and executives, as well as internal sales and SE teams
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence without direct authority
  • Comfortable managing multiple partners and opportunities simultaneously across different stages of the sales cycle
  • Data-driven approach to pipeline management, forecasting, and partner performance tracking
  • Track record of driving outcomes through enablement, joint execution, and consistent partner engagement
  • Customer-centric mindset, with a focus on driving successful adoption and long-term value through partners

Postman hires company builders. You enjoy building partner relationships, creating repeatable motions, and helping scale a high-impact channel business as part of one of the best companies in the world.

What Else?

In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.

At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.

Our Values

At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. We value transparency and honest communication about not only successes, but also failures. In our work, we focus on specific goals that add up to a larger vision. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. We are dedicated to delivering the best products we can.

Equal opportunity

Postman is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. Postman does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Postman will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Postman.

The candidate's background in closing complex deals and scaling partner ecosystems across multiple regions complements Postman's global reach and focus on collaboration in the API space. However, the candidate may need to familiarize themselves with the specific nuances of the API lifecycle and Postman's unique platform offerings to fully leverage their skills in this role.

title: Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager
descriptionhtml:
We are currently recruiting for an Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager for possibly one of the most exciting businesses we have partnered with in the past 12 months.


This is a newly created role for an organisation that is scaling at a phenomenal pace, leading their DACH (Germany, Austria & Switzerland) territory, headquartered in Berlin.


With an unparalleled culture favouring independence and speed over perfection, the successful Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager will work with best-in-class leadership and unlock unlimited opportunities for personal, career, and financial growth.


The organisation’s biggest challenge is acquiring a reliable supply of inventory stock, and the Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager will focus on this challenge with incredibly clear goals.


The successful candidate will take ownership of the organisation’s most strategic partnerships across leasing providers, OEMs, and major retail groups, which will drive the largest share of the organisation’s supply.


Working on the clear goal of securing high-quality and predictable inflows of inventory through agreements with major industry players, the Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager will spend 35% of their time developing existing relationships with the remainder on building new relationships


You will work with sophisticated partners who expect senior-level engagement, clear business cases, and fast, professional processes.


The day-to-day duties will include:

  • Strengthening and expanding the business’s strategic B2B partnerships across DACH
  • Managing a large portfolio of key retail chains and OEMs, taking ownership of and growing strategic partnerships, building trust-based relationships, and becoming the key contact for all commercial topics
  • Collaborating closely with operations, product, pricing, logistics, and leadership teams to translate partnerships into scalable inventory growth for the business
  • Leading complex commercial negotiations to form framework agreements, secure favourable purchasing conditions, and ensure alignment across volumes, pricing, and service levels
  • Taking ownership of senior stakeholder relationships to drive recurring volumes, expand to additional locations, and ensure partners view you and the business as their primary resale channel
  • Immersing in companywide and warehouse operations, troubleshooting and building enhanced processes as the operation continues to expand
  • Using data to lead decisions, including working with forecasts, ROI cases, and KPIs to prioritise and manage performance
  • Identifying new commercial levers such as joint campaigns and affiliate models to unlock additional stock volume
  • Enabling a strong partner experience with smooth onboarding, reliable collection of inventory, and an exceptional level of customer service


What we are looking for:

  • 5+ years of experience within business development, enterprise sales, key account management, or strategic buying
  • Robust stakeholder management skills, with a proven track record of delivering results across complex and long sales cycles and building high-level credibility with senior stakeholders
  • An excellent track record of securing structured agreements which balance partner value with the business’s economics
  • Fluent English and German written and verbal communication skills


Why you should consider this business:

  • Join a rapidly scaling business that has already secured Series C funding, backed by top-tier investors during a decisive expansion phase
  • Earn a competitive basic salary in addition to a joining bonus and an annual bonus offered in the form of stock options. The business is set to explode over the coming 5 years, and anyone with stock options is set to receive life-changing compensation
  • Work in a role with a high level of strategic impact, shaping the most important sourcing channels in the company’s largest market
  • Be part of an organisation already established internationally, with offices across Europe and the US
  • The sky is the limit for your own personal growth. Upwards mobility is a key feature of this business’s culture, with multiple examples of internal promotion in a rapidly scaling, entrepreneurial environment


If this role sounds like a strong fit for your background and skillset, please contact t.waterman@gs2partnership.com today.

title: Head of Strategic Partnerships (m/w/d) COO Track
descriptionhtml:
Join PsyCurio and help shape the future of digital health and mental well-being. We develop evidence-based digital solutions that measurably improve mental performance and sustainable well-being.


With proven market success in Germany and Switzerland, partnerships with health insurers, and collaborations with leading international research institutions, we are entering our next growth phase. Supported by the German Accelerator program in the US, we are expanding globally — and are looking for a key person to drive this journey.


This role offers a clear path toward a COO position, ideal for entrepreneurial individuals who want to actively shape a company rather than just work within it.


Tasks

  • Build, develop, and manage strategic partnerships (e.g., health insurers, digital platforms, international partners)


Identify and unlock new growth and collaboration opportunities


Support fundraising activities and investor relations


Develop and execute go-to-market strategies together with partners


Drive and expand business development initiatives


Collaborate closely with product, operations, and the founding team


Contribute to international expansion (DACH, US, global)


Help shape the long-term strategic direction of the company


Requirements

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong sense of ownership and responsibility


Experience or strong interest in business development, partnerships, or strategy


Ability to build and maintain long-term relationships with stakeholders


Strong analytical and structured working style


Excellent communication and negotiation skills


Willingness and potential to take on leadership responsibility early


Fluent in English and German


Passion for digital health, psychology, or healthcare innovation


Based in Berlin or willing to relocate (remote within Germany possible)You are self-reliant and responsible


Benefits

COO Track: Clear development path toward an operational leadership role or co-founder position

Equity participation: Long-term upside and ownership in the company

High impact: Direct influence on strategy, partnerships, and growth from day one

Startup environment: Fast-paced, dynamic, and mission-driven team

Learning & exposure: Insights into fundraising, international scaling, and leadership

Flexibility: Remote work options and flexible working hours

Flat hierarchies: Short decision-making paths and real ownership

Creative freedom: Space to bring in your own ideas and shape processes


We encourage young MBA graduates/graduates to apply for this position as well as experienced leaders!

Based in Berlin or willing to relocate (remote within Germany possible)You are self-reliant and responsible


Benefits

COO Track: Clear development path toward an operational leadership role or co-founder position

Equity participation: Long-term upside and ownership in the company

High impact: Direct influence on strategy, partnerships, and growth from day one

Startup environment: Fast-paced, dynamic, and mission-driven team

Learning & exposure: Insights into fundraising, international scaling, and leadership

Flexibility: Remote work options and flexible working hours

Flat hierarchies: Short decision-making paths and real ownership

Creative freedom: Space to bring in your own ideas and shape processes


We encourage young MBA graduates/graduates to apply for this position as well as experienced leaders!

title: Business Development Manager (London)
descriptionhtml:
The AI-powered OS for beauty,

wellness and self-care

About Fresha

Fresha is the AI-powered operating system for the global beauty, wellness and self-care industry, connecting and powering everything from salons and barbers to spas, medspas, fitness studios and health practices.

Trusted by millions of consumers and businesses worldwide. Fresha is used by 140,000+ businesses and 450,000+ stylists and professionals worldwide, processing over 1 billion appointments to date.

The company is headquartered in London, United Kingdom, with 15 global offices located across North America, EMEA and APAC.

Fresha allows consumers to discover, book and pay for beauty and wellness appointments with local businesses via its marketplace, while beauty and wellness businesses and professionals use an all-in-one platform to manage their entire operations with an intuitive business software and financial technology solutions.

Fresha’s ecosystem gives merchants everything they need to run their business seamlessly by facilitating appointment bookings, point-of-sale, customer records management, marketing automation, loyalty, beauty products inventory and team management.

The consumer marketplace unlocks revenue potential for partner businesses by leveraging the power of online bookings and automated marketing through mobile apps and advanced integrations with major tech brands including Instagram, Facebook and Google.

Role Overview

Given our exciting and progressive growth plans, we are looking for an exceptional Business Development Manager to come and join our global business.

Reporting directly to the Head of Sales (Europe), and working collaboratively with the Commercial team and potential partners, you will be 100% focused on bringing in new business development.

This is a great opportunity for someone looking to work in a fast-paced and changing environment, who likes to work autonomously, enjoys a challenge and wants to make an impact.

To foster a collaborative environment that thrives on face-to-face interactions and teamwork, all Fresha employees work from our dog-friendly office five days per week. Our London HQ: The Bower, 207-211, Old St, Tower, London EC1V 9NR

What You Will Be Doing

  • KPIs - Consistently achieve sales targets
  • Outreach - Calling key decision-makers using phone, email & social touches with the goal of bringing on new business (there may be some in-person meetings as appropriate)
  • Selling - Present, promote and sell products and services through product demos to prospective and existing partners
  • Sales Cycle - Managing the entire sales process from prospecting to close
  • Partnering - Perform cost-benefit and needs analysis with existing/potential partners in order to align our solutions to their needs and business objectives
  • Collaboration - Work closely with other departments to ensure a seamless partner experience
  • Market Trends - Provide feedback about ongoing trends in the industry
  • Leads - Contact 100% of qualified inbound leads in a timely manner, and document all call outcomes in the CRM
  • Business Partnering - Serve as a lead point of contact for partners on all commercial activities

This list is not exhaustive and there may be other activities you are required to deliver.

What We Are Looking For

  • Experience - Minimum 2 years of solid B2B sales with a track record of success
  • Industry - SaaS, online marketplaces, or payment platforms is highly desirable
  • Relationship Building - You are a true hunter and relationship builder
  • Organised - You can multitask, prioritise and manage time effectively
  • Curiosity - You are naturally curious and have a desire to continually grow and develop

Added bonus

  • Bi-lingual - Fluency in English and another language
  • Beauty & Wellness - Experience within the beauty and wellness industry

At Fresha, we value passion and potential as much as specific skills. If you're enthusiastic and eager to learn, we encourage you to apply, even if you don't meet every listed requirement. We believe in fostering growth and providing the support needed for you to excel in your role.

Interview process

  • Screen Call - Video-call with a member from the Talent Team (30 mins)
  • Skills & Experience - Video/In-person interview with Team Leads (30 mins)
  • Presentation - In-person interview with General Manager (UK & Ireland) and Team Leads (60 mins)

We aim to finalise the entire interview process and deliver feedback within 2 weeks.

Every job application received is reviewed manually by our talent team. While we strive to assess applications within 7 days, the sheer volume of talented individuals expressing interest may occasionally extend this timeframe.

Inclusive workforce

At Fresha, we are creating a culture where individuals of all backgrounds feel comfortable.

We want all Fresha people to feel included and truly empowered to contribute fully to our vision and goals. Everyone who applies will receive fair consideration for employment.

We do not discriminate based on race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, marital status, gender identity, national origin, disability, or any other applicable legally protected characteristics in the location in which the candidate is applying.

If you have any accessibility requirements that would make you more comfortable during the interview process and/or once you join, please let us know so that we can support you.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Partner Manager (f/m/x)
jobfunction: Business Development, Project Management, and Strategy/Planning
descriptionhtml:
About lemon.markets 🍋


We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.


lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.


As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.


Your Mission:


At lemon.markets, you’ll own and grow partner/customer relationships end-to-end, from onboarding to launch, day-to-day operations, and expansion. As our Senior Partner Manager, you’re the partner’s main point of contact and their strongest internal advocate, orchestrating cross-functional teams to help partners build successful investment products on top of lemon.markets.

Our partner universe is currently evolving: alongside our direct business, where we typically work with fintechs and neobanks, we were recently acquired by dwpbank and are now building a combined offering with them. This means we’ll be welcoming a new wave of partners, with a strong focus on German banking partners, who will onboard through the joint motion and be supported by Partner Management from day one.

Your role is to make this journey feel customer-centric, structured, and straightforward, while ensuring we meet the regulatory and operational standards required to scale safely and sustainably.


You will:


  • Own customer onboarding: Take partners from handover/intake to go-live by coordinating internal workstreams and driving timelines, decisions, and execution.
  • Lead day-to-day partner management: Act as the single point of contact post-launch, run partner cadences, manage escalations, and represent partner needs internally.
  • Ensure regulatory & outsourcing readiness: Drive third-party risk, compliance, and outsourcing requirements end-to-end and help define/manage SLAs where relevant.
  • Drive partner growth: Identify expansion opportunities, translate them into concrete initiatives, and validate business cases to grow partner value and revenue.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to improve product-market fit: Partner closely with Go-to-Market, Product, Brokerage, and Operations to feed insights back into our product and services.
  • Build and scale the Partner Management function: Develop frameworks, playbooks, and standards, and document learnings to continuously improve how we work.


You will be successful in this role if:


  • Bring extensive experience in B2B Partner Management, Onboarding, or Project/Program Management, and you’re used to taking clear ownership of cross-functional delivery, driving outcomes beyond your own team.
  • Have experience in the financial industry, ideally in a brokerage, brokerage-related environment, fintech, or banking context and you can quickly build a strong understanding of our business.
  • Have experience managing a diverse partner portfolio across fast-moving fintechs/neobanks and (larger) German banks/corporate banks, adjusting your style to different decision-making cultures and stakeholder landscapes.
  • Have hands-on exposure to regulatory environments and have worked closely with Risk, Compliance, and Outsourcing / vendor management functions to keep delivery smooth while staying within the guardrails.
  • Understand end-to-end partner management and enjoy owning the full lifecycle: onboarding → launch → day to day management → growth/expansion.
  • Communicate exceptionally well in English and German (C1+) building trust quickly, leading tough conversations calmly, and keeping momentum across senior internal and external stakeholders.


How we take care of you:


  • 🚀 Compensation package: We offer you a competitive salary, based on your experience and the value-add you bring to lemon.markets.
  • 🕑 Flexible working hours: We trust people to choose when they work best and avoid a rigid 9 to 5 mindset. You decide on the rhythm that helps you do your best work, with only shared core hours from 12:00 to 16:00 to support collaboration.
  • 🏢 Hybrid work setup: You can work from anywhere in Germany within a hybrid model. At the same time, in-person collaboration is a key part of our culture. That being said, individual contributer lemoneers work 4 days or more per month on-site in our Berlin office, spending quality time together with the team at events, onsites/offsites or over team dinners.
  • 📈 Grow with us: We take your personal development seriously! You’ll get regular mentorship and stretch opportunities, a clear career development framework with frequent feedback conversations as well as an annual learning budget.
  • 🌱 People & culture: You’ll feel our culture in the small things, like polaroids from our lemoneers’ first day, Friday food market strolls at Maybachufer, and our monthly new-joiner breakfast. But also in the bigger things: celebrating the onboarding of new partners, growing our teams, and owning a strong discussion culture.
  • 💛 Perks & benefits: We choose benefits that add actual value. We sponsor a Deutschlandticket for employees on unlimited contracts, plus 50% off on Beatvest - our partner for financial education and investing.
  • 🏝 Workation: Get out of here! At lemon.markets you can work remotely from anywhere in the EU for up to 10 consecutive days at a time, and up to 60 days per year in total.


As an equal opportunity employer, lemon.markets is committed to building an inclusive workplace. The company welcomes people from all different backgrounds, including age, citizenship, ethnic and racial origins, gender identities, individuals with disabilities, marital status, religions and ideologies, and sexual orientations to apply.


If you feel this role could be a great match for you even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you, please apply.


You have any questions? Feel free to contact us via talent@lemon.markets.com

description_html:
About lemon.markets 🍋


We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.


lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.


As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.


Your Mission:


At lemon.markets, you’ll own and grow partner/customer relationships end-to-end, from onboarding to launch, day-to-day operations, and expansion. As our Senior Partner Manager, you’re the partner’s main point of contact and their strongest internal advocate, orchestrating cross-functional teams to help partners build successful investment products on top of lemon.markets.

Our partner universe is currently evolving: alongside our direct business, where we typically work with fintechs and neobanks, we were recently acquired by dwpbank and are now building a combined offering with them. This means we’ll be welcoming a new wave of partners, with a strong focus on German banking partners, who will onboard through the joint motion and be supported by Partner Management from day one.

Your role is to make this journey feel customer-centric, structured, and straightforward, while ensuring we meet the regulatory and operational standards required to scale safely and sustainably.


You will:


  • Own customer onboarding: Take partners from handover/intake to go-live by coordinating internal workstreams and driving timelines, decisions, and execution.
  • Lead day-to-day partner management: Act as the single point of contact post-launch, run partner cadences, manage escalations, and represent partner needs internally.
  • Ensure regulatory & outsourcing readiness: Drive third-party risk, compliance, and outsourcing requirements end-to-end and help define/manage SLAs where relevant.
  • Drive partner growth: Identify expansion opportunities, translate them into concrete initiatives, and validate business cases to grow partner value and revenue.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to improve product-market fit: Partner closely with Go-to-Market, Product, Brokerage, and Operations to feed insights back into our product and services.
  • Build and scale the Partner Management function: Develop frameworks, playbooks, and standards, and document learnings to continuously improve how we work.


You will be successful in this role if:


  • Bring extensive experience in B2B Partner Management, Onboarding, or Project/Program Management, and you’re used to taking clear ownership of cross-functional delivery, driving outcomes beyond your own team.
  • Have experience in the financial industry, ideally in a brokerage, brokerage-related environment, fintech, or banking context and you can quickly build a strong understanding of our business.
  • Have experience managing a diverse partner portfolio across fast-moving fintechs/neobanks and (larger) German banks/corporate banks, adjusting your style to different decision-making cultures and stakeholder landscapes.
  • Have hands-on exposure to regulatory environments and have worked closely with Risk, Compliance, and Outsourcing / vendor management functions to keep delivery smooth while staying within the guardrails.
  • Understand end-to-end partner management and enjoy owning the full lifecycle: onboarding → launch → day to day management → growth/expansion.
  • Communicate exceptionally well in English and German (C1+) building trust quickly, leading tough conversations calmly, and keeping momentum across senior internal and external stakeholders.


How we take care of you:


  • 🚀 Compensation package: We offer you a competitive salary, based on your experience and the value-add you bring to lemon.markets.
  • 🕑 Flexible working hours: We trust people to choose when they work best and avoid a rigid 9 to 5 mindset. You decide on the rhythm that helps you do your best work, with only shared core hours from 12:00 to 16:00 to support collaboration.
  • 🏢 Hybrid work setup: You can work from anywhere in Germany within a hybrid model. At the same time, in-person collaboration is a key part of our culture. That being said, individual contributer lemoneers work 4 days or more per month on-site in our Berlin office, spending quality time together with the team at events, onsites/offsites or over team dinners.
  • 📈 Grow with us: We take your personal development seriously! You’ll get regular mentorship and stretch opportunities, a clear career development framework with frequent feedback conversations as well as an annual learning budget.
  • 🌱 People & culture: You’ll feel our culture in the small things, like polaroids from our lemoneers’ first day, Friday food market strolls at Maybachufer, and our monthly new-joiner breakfast. But also in the bigger things: celebrating the onboarding of new partners, growing our teams, and owning a strong discussion culture.
  • 💛 Perks & benefits: We choose benefits that add actual value. We sponsor a Deutschlandticket for employees on unlimited contracts, plus 50% off on Beatvest - our partner for financial education and investing.
  • 🏝 Workation: Get out of here! At lemon.markets you can work remotely from anywhere in the EU for up to 10 consecutive days at a time, and up to 60 days per year in total.


As an equal opportunity employer, lemon.markets is committed to building an inclusive workplace. The company welcomes people from all different backgrounds, including age, citizenship, ethnic and racial origins, gender identities, individuals with disabilities, marital status, religions and ideologies, and sexual orientations to apply.


If you feel this role could be a great match for you even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you, please apply.


You have any questions? Feel free to contact us via talent@lemon.markets.com
reason: Focuses on managing B2B partner relationships in the financial services sector.
fit_explanation: This job is a good fit for the candidate due to their extensive experience in enterprise technology sales and their proven ability to build go-to-market strategies, which aligns well with lemon.markets' focus on empowering FinTechs and banks. The candidate's background in closing complex deals and scaling partner ecosystems across various regions complements the role's requirement for managing partnerships effectively. However, there may be a gap in direct experience with brokerage services and financial products, which could be crucial for understanding the specific needs of lemon.markets' partners.
